worth_it_explanation: Optimized targeting replaced audience expansion in DV360 and works differently from the predecessor it replaced. It extends a line item's reach beyond the seed audience Google receives, which is useful when prospecting but counterproductive when the seed is a carefully built first-party list and precision matters more than scale. A line item where optimized targeting is on by default and no one has reviewed that setting can be spending against users far outside the intended audience. The check takes a few minutes per line item and the correction is a single toggle.
intro: Optimized targeting is a line-item-level setting in Display and Video 360 (DV360) that allows Google's system to bid on users outside the seed audience segments attached to the line item when those users are predicted to convert at a similar rate. It replaced audience expansion in 2022 and is available on display, video, and connected TV (CTV) line items. These checks confirm that optimized targeting is enabled or disabled intentionally based on the line item goal, that it is not overriding a narrow first-party seed in a way that defeats the targeting strategy, and that the seed audience passed to the system is structured to give the algorithm a useful signal when expansion is the goal.

audit_checks.title: Optimized targeting is on for a line item whose goal requires reaching a specific known audience
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A retargeting or high-intent first-party audience line item with optimized targeting on will spend a share of its budget against users Google predicts to convert, not against the seed list the advertiser built. The line item delivers, but a portion of impressions reaches users outside the intended audience. For a suppression list or a past-purchaser exclusion use case, this can mean serving ads to users who should not see them.

educate.base:
Optimized targeting gives Display and Video 360 (DV360) permission to bid on users outside the seed audience attached to a line item when Google's prediction model expects those users to convert. When prospecting, this is the intended behavior. When the goal is to reach a specific, known group, such as cart abandoners, past purchasers, or a Customer Match list, optimized targeting works against the precision the audience was built to provide.

The setting defaults to on for new line items as of 2022, when Google replaced audience expansion with optimized targeting. An account that has not reviewed line items created after that transition may have retargeting line items running with the setting on without anyone having made that choice deliberately.

The impact is not always visible in delivery metrics. The line item reaches its frequency goals and spends its budget. The shift is in who receives those impressions: a share goes to users Google selected rather than users on the list. For frequency-capped retargeting, this means members of the seed list see fewer ads and non-members see some, which is the opposite of what retargeting is meant to do.

A few signs point to this situation:
  • The line item targets a first-party audience list or a Customer Match segment and was created after mid-2022
  • The optimized targeting toggle in the line item settings shows as on
  • Reach in reporting is broader than the known size of the seed audience would support at the line item's frequency cap
You may want to review the optimized targeting setting on each line item that targets a first-party list before assuming the audience boundaries are holding.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a retail advertiser account, cart-abandonment and past-purchaser line items are the most directly affected. A cart-abandonment campaign with optimized targeting on will serve some impressions to users who never visited the cart. You may want to confirm the setting is off on line items whose purpose is to re-engage a specific group of known site visitors.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation advertiser account, CRM-based Customer Match line items built for lead nurture are the most sensitive to this setting. A line item built to reach contacts at a specific stage of a sales process may expand to users who match the conversion pattern but are not in the intended list. You may want to confirm the setting matches whether the goal is to reach the specific list or to find users like them.

learn_more.source_title: Set up targeting for a line item

impactful_updates.update_summary: Google replaced audience expansion with optimized targeting in DV360. The new setting is on by default for eligible line items. Line items that previously had audience expansion off were not automatically migrated to the equivalent off state for optimized targeting in all cases.

investigate.intro: Check the optimized targeting setting on each line item that targets a first-party audience, a Customer Match segment, or any other list where precision is the goal.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the advertiser, navigate to 'Line items', and filter to active line items.
Open a line item that targets a first-party audience list or a Customer Match segment.
In the line item settings, find the 'Optimized targeting' section under the audience targeting area and note whether the toggle is on or off.
Repeat for each line item where the campaign goal requires reaching the seed audience specifically rather than expanding beyond it.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per line item with a first-party or Customer Match seed audience, whether the campaign goal is retargeting or prospecting and whether optimized targeting is on or off. A prospecting line item with the setting on passes. A retargeting line item or suppression-adjacent line item with the setting on is a fail recorded with the line item name and the audience segment it targets.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the optimized targeting status and line item goal for each first-party audience line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that optimized targeting is on for line items on {{site_name}} that target first-party or Customer Match audiences whose purpose is to reach a specific known group rather than to prospect for new users. When optimized targeting is on, DV360 bids on users outside the seed list that it predicts will convert, which diverts impressions away from the intended audience. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing each retargeting and audience-specific line item and turning optimized targeting off where the goal is to reach the seed audience rather than to expand beyond it.

audit_checks.title: Optimized targeting is on for prospecting but the seed audience is too narrow to give the algorithm a useful signal
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When optimized targeting runs from a seed that is too small or too loosely defined, Google has insufficient data to identify the conversion pattern it should expand toward. The line item broadens delivery but without a clear directional signal, so it reaches a wider audience with lower relevance than a well-seeded expansion would produce.

educate.base:
Optimized targeting in Display and Video 360 (DV360) uses the seed audience attached to a line item as a signal to identify users with a similar conversion pattern. The quality of that signal depends on the seed. A seed audience with fewer than a few hundred members gives the model limited data to work from. A seed built from a broad Google affinity category rather than a first-party list gives the model a weak intent signal because affinity segments describe interests, not conversion behavior.

When prospecting with optimized targeting, the most productive seed is a first-party list of converted users, such as a Floodlight-based purchaser list, or a Customer Match list of existing customers. These audiences represent the conversion event the line item is trying to replicate. A seed built from a contextual category or a broad in-market segment tells the model something about the content a user reads but less about whether they convert.

There is no minimum seed size enforced by DV360 for optimized targeting to run, but Google's guidance on optimized targeting notes that a richer seed produces more relevant expansion. A seed that is very small or built from broad behavioral categories is not a configuration error, but it is worth reviewing when the expanded audience's conversion rate does not meet expectations.

A few signs point to a seed that may not be giving the algorithm enough to work with:
  • The seed audience attached to the line item has fewer than 500 members
  • The seed is a Google affinity or generic in-market segment rather than a first-party list
  • The line item is running optimized targeting but post-click conversion rates from the expanded audience trail the account average by a wide margin
You may want to confirm the seed is built from users who have already completed the goal the campaign is optimizing toward.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a retail advertiser account, the strongest seed for a prospecting line item using optimized targeting is a Floodlight-based purchaser list or a Customer Match upload of existing buyers. You may want to confirm the purchase-confirmation Floodlight tag is active and that the resulting list has enough members to give the model a meaningful conversion signal before relying on optimized targeting for prospecting.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation advertiser account, the most useful seed is a Floodlight-based list of users who completed a form submission or a Customer Match list of converted leads. A seed built only from page-visit data covers a broader pool that includes users who did not convert, which gives the model a weaker signal. You may want to confirm the seed reflects the conversion event, not just site engagement.

investigate.intro: For each prospecting line item with optimized targeting on, check the seed audience's type, size, and whether it reflects the conversion event the campaign is optimizing toward.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the line item and navigate to its audience targeting section. Note which audience lists are attached as the seed.
Open Audience Manager for the advertiser and check the size of each seed audience list. Note lists with fewer than 500 members.
For each seed audience, note whether it is a first-party Floodlight-based list, a Customer Match list, or a Google audience segment such as an affinity or in-market category.

In the campaign's performance data, compare the conversion rate from the portion of delivery that reached users outside the seed (the expanded audience) against the overall account conversion rate for similar line items.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per prospecting line item with optimized targeting on, the seed audience type, its size, and whether it is built from conversion events. A line item with a first-party conversion-based seed of more than 500 members passes. A line item with a seed below 500 members or built entirely from Google affinity or in-market segments is a flag recorded with the line item name and the seed audience details.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the seed audience type, size, and composition for each prospecting line item with optimized targeting on above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more prospecting line items on {{site_name}} are running optimized targeting with a seed audience that may not give the algorithm a strong enough conversion signal. When the seed is very small or built from broad behavioral categories rather than first-party conversion data, DV360's expansion reaches a wider audience without a clear directional signal, which can reduce the relevance of the users the line item reaches. {{voice_recommend}} updating the seed audience on affected line items to a first-party list of users who have completed the campaign's conversion goal, such as a Floodlight-based purchaser list or a Customer Match upload of existing customers, so the algorithm has a well-defined conversion pattern to expand from.

audit_checks.title: Optimized targeting is off for a prospecting line item where it would extend reach toward likely converters
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A prospecting line item with optimized targeting off reaches only users in the attached audience segments. When the seed is a first-party list of converters and the goal is to find new users with a similar pattern, leaving optimized targeting off means the line item delivers exclusively to the seed rather than expanding toward users who resemble it. The missed scale can be recovered by turning the setting on.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 (DV360) allows a line item to target only the users in its attached seed audience or to use those users as a signal and extend bidding to users outside the seed who share a similar conversion pattern. When optimized targeting is off, the line item delivers only to users in the seed segments. For a prospecting line item whose goal is to reach new users who resemble existing converters, this limits the campaign to the seed itself, which is the opposite of prospecting.

The most productive configuration for a prospecting line item uses a first-party conversion-based seed, such as a Floodlight purchaser list, and sets optimized targeting on. The algorithm reads the seed, identifies the characteristics associated with conversion in this account, and extends bids to users outside the list who match that pattern. A line item configured this way reaches beyond the seed without requiring the advertiser to define every targeting attribute manually.

A prospecting line item with optimized targeting off is not necessarily misconfigured. A line item deliberately built to serve only to a specific audience layer, such as a contextual placement with no audience requirement, may leave optimized targeting off intentionally. The check is whether the intent matches the setting.

A few signs point to optimized targeting being off when it could help:
  • The line item is labeled as prospecting or new-user acquisition in its naming convention
  • The campaign goal is to drive conversions from users who have not interacted with the advertiser before
  • The line item's seed audience is a first-party purchaser or converter list rather than a broad behavioral category
  • Optimized targeting shows as off in the line item settings
You may want to confirm the setting matches the prospecting intent before leaving it off.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a retail advertiser account, a new-customer acquisition line item that targets a lookalike or similar-audience strategy by keeping optimized targeting off is leaving available scale on the table. You may want to confirm whether the line item is intentionally restricted to the seed or whether enabling optimized targeting would serve the new-customer acquisition goal.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation advertiser account, a prospecting line item with a converted-lead seed and optimized targeting off will deliver only to users already on the converted-lead list, which is a retargeting use case, not prospecting. You may want to confirm whether the intent is to reach the list or to find new users who resemble it.

investigate.intro: For each line item with a prospecting or new-user-acquisition goal, confirm whether optimized targeting is on and whether the setting matches the campaign intent.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the insertion order and review the campaign goal and line item naming to identify which line items have a prospecting or new-user acquisition intent.
Open each prospecting line item and navigate to the audience targeting section. Confirm whether optimized targeting is on or off.

For line items with the setting off, note the seed audience attached. If the seed is a first-party conversion list and the goal is to reach new users who resemble converters, the setting may not match the intent.
Review the line item's reach metrics to assess whether delivery is constrained to the seed size or extending beyond it.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per prospecting line item, whether optimized targeting is on or off and whether the setting matches the line item goal. A prospecting line item with optimized targeting on and a first-party conversion-based seed passes. A prospecting line item with optimized targeting off and a goal that requires expanding beyond the seed is a flag recorded with the line item name, the seed audience, and the stated goal.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the optimized targeting status, seed audience, and campaign goal for each prospecting line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more prospecting line items on {{site_name}} have optimized targeting off, which restricts delivery to users already in the seed audience rather than extending reach to users who share the same conversion pattern. For a line item whose goal is to acquire new users who resemble existing converters, this configuration limits the campaign to the seed itself. {{voice_recommend}} enabling optimized targeting on affected prospecting line items that use a first-party conversion-based seed, so DV360 can extend bidding to users outside the seed who match the conversion pattern the algorithm identifies.
